chapter_title.en: Chapter 67 – The self-sufficiency from the people

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (354/ 8)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (354/ 8)

parts.text: عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ ( صلوات الله عليه )

text: رَأَيْتُ الْخَيْرَ كُلَّهُ قَدِ اجْتَمَعَ فِي قَطْعِ الطَّمَعِ عَمَّا فِي أَيْدِي النَّاسِ وَ مَنْ لَمْ يَرْجُ النَّاسَ فِي شَيْ‏ءٍ وَ رَدَّ أَمْرَهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي جَمِيعِ أُمُورِهِ اسْتَجَابَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَهُ فِي كُلِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ .

en.hubeali: And by this chain, from Minqary, from Abdul Razzaq, from Moamar, from Al Zahry,

(It has been narrated) from Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> saw the goodness, all of it having gathered in cutting off the greed from what is in the hands of the people; and the one who does not hope to the people regarding anything and refers his matter to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic in the entirety of his matters, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would Answer to him with regards to everything’.
en.sarwar: 3. Through the same chain of narrators it is narrated from al-Minqari from ‘Abd al- Razzaq from Mu’ammar from al-Zuhri from Ali ibn al-Husayn (a.s.) who has said the following: “I saw all good gathered together in cutting off of yearning for what belongs to people and in not having any hope in people in anything and in leaving his affairs in the hands of Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, in all matters. Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, will answer him in all things.”
